data.title: Fed's Stance on Oil Shocks Lowers Rate Hike Odds for 2026
data.description: U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated that the central bank is currently overlooking short-term oil price fluctuations, which has contributed to a decrease in the likelihood of a rate hike in 2026 from 25% to 5%. According to NS3.AI, Powell's remarks led to a decline in U.S. Treasury yields, although West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil prices increased by 5.3%, reaching nearly $105 per barrel. This rise in oil prices exerted pressure on both stock markets and Bitcoin.

data.title: Oil Prices Reach Highest Level Since July 2022 Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: Oil prices have surged to their highest point since July 2022, as the conflict involving Iran extends into its fifth week. CNN Breaking News posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions on global energy markets. The prolonged conflict has disrupted supply chains and heightened concerns over potential shortages, driving prices upward. Analysts suggest that the situation could lead to further volatility in the oil market, with potential implications for global economic stability. As the situation develops, stakeholders are closely monitoring the geopolitical landscape and its effects on energy prices.

data.title: S&P 500 Recovers $900 Billion Amid US-Iran Talks
data.description: The S&P 500 saw a recovery of approximately $900 billion in market capitalization by Monday morning. According to NS3.AI, this rebound followed weekend developments involving Iran and Washington that led to a significant reversal in futures. U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the United States is engaged in discussions with what he described as a new and more reasonable regime in Iran to halt military operations. However, no formal agreement has been finalized.

data.title: Federal Indictment: Jonathan Spalletta Charged in Uranium Finance Hacks
data.description: Federal prosecutors have charged Jonathan Spalletta in connection with two alleged hacks targeting Uranium Finance. According to NS3.AI, the indictment accuses Spalletta of executing one attack that resulted in the theft of $53.3 million. The charges include one count of computer fraud and one count of money laundering, which together could lead to a maximum sentence of 30 years in prison. Prosecutors noted that the subsequent exploit left Uranium Finance without sufficient funds to maintain operations.

data.title: Fed's Williams Projects 2.5% GDP Growth for U.S. This Year
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is overseeing an economy that is expected to grow by 2.5% this year, according to New York Federal Reserve President John Williams. According to Jin10, Williams attributes this growth to a variety of factors that are positively influencing the U.S. economy. He emphasized the importance of these elements in driving economic expansion, although specific details on these factors were not disclosed. Williams' projection aligns with the Federal Reserve's broader outlook for the U.S. economy, which has shown resilience amid global uncertainties. The Fed continues to monitor economic indicators closely to ensure sustainable growth.

data.title: Fed's Williams: Inflation Expected to Rise Due to War and Tariffs
data.description: According to Jin10, Federal Reserve official John Williams has indicated that the overall inflation rate is anticipated to increase in the short term. This expectation is attributed to the ongoing impacts of war and tariffs. Williams highlighted that these factors are likely to exert upward pressure on prices, affecting the economic landscape. The statement underscores the challenges faced by policymakers in managing inflation amid geopolitical tensions and trade barriers.

data.title: Fed's Williams Predicts Slight Decline in Unemployment Over Next Two Years
data.description: According to Jin10, Federal Reserve official John Williams has forecasted a modest decrease in the unemployment rate over the next two years. Williams, who serves as the President of the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, shared his insights during a recent discussion on economic trends. He emphasized that while the labor market is expected to improve, the pace of change may be gradual. Williams' comments come amid ongoing debates about the U.S. economic outlook and the Federal Reserve's monetary policy strategies. His predictions align with broader expectations of steady economic growth, although challenges remain in achieving significant reductions in unemployment. The Federal Reserve continues to monitor various economic indicators to guide its policy decisions.

data.title: Iran Denies Negotiations with U.S. Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs has stated that no negotiations have taken place with the United States during the 31 days of the ongoing conflict. According to NS3.AI, this announcement comes amid heightened tensions and ongoing hostilities, with both nations maintaining their respective stances. The lack of dialogue underscores the complexities and challenges in resolving the conflict diplomatically.

data.title: Bitcoin Fear and Greed Index Hits Record Low Amid Prolonged Pessimism
data.description: The Bitcoin Fear and Greed Index has dropped to 8, marking a significant period of market pessimism. According to NS3.AI, the index has remained below 25, indicating Extreme Fear, for 59 consecutive days. This represents the longest continuous stretch of negative sentiment since the collapse of FTX in late 2022.

data.title: U.S. May Reevaluate NATO Ties Post-Iran Conflict, Says Rubio
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has suggested that the United States might need to reconsider its relationship with NATO following the conclusion of the ongoing conflict with Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Rubio's remarks as part of a broader discussion on U.S. foreign policy and international alliances. Rubio emphasized the importance of evaluating strategic partnerships to ensure they align with current geopolitical realities. The statement comes amid heightened tensions and evolving global dynamics, prompting discussions on the future of international alliances and the role of the U.S. in global security frameworks.

data.title: Banco do Brasil Considers Financial Relief for Farmers Affected by Iran Conflict
data.description: Banco do Brasil is contemplating new financial support measures for farmers impacted by the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this initiative could potentially strain the bank's balance sheet. The move may also necessitate asset sales or a capital increase to maintain financial stability.

data.title: Mitsubishi to Utilize JPMorgan's Kinexys for Global Fund Transfers
data.description: Mitsubishi has announced plans to leverage JPMorgan's Kinexys network for its global corporate fund transfers. According to NS3.AI, the Kinexys platform has processed over $3 trillion since its inception in 2020. JPMorgan is targeting an increase in daily transactions to $10 billion, up from the current average of $7 billion. Additionally, JPMorgan is working on Kinexys Fund Flow, a system designed for tokenized assets, with a rollout anticipated later this year.

data.title: Israel Enacts Law Permitting Execution for Deadly Attacks
data.description: Israel has passed a controversial law allowing the execution of Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks. New York Times posted on X, highlighting that critics argue the law may not be equally applied to Jewish extremists found guilty of similar offenses. The legislation has sparked significant debate and concern over its potential implications and fairness in application. Observers are closely monitoring the situation to see how the law will be enforced and its impact on the region's already tense dynamics.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 610 USDT with a Narrowed 0.10% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 30, 2026, 19:08 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 610 USDT and is now trading at 609.830017 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.10% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: U.S. Oil Prices Surpass $100 Amid Conflict with Iran
data.description: U.S. oil prices have closed above $100 per barrel, marking a significant milestone since the onset of the conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions on the energy market. The escalation in hostilities has raised concerns over potential disruptions in oil supply, contributing to the surge in prices. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation as it unfolds, with implications for global energy security and economic stability. The market response underscores the sensitivity of oil prices to geopolitical developments, particularly in regions critical to global supply chains.

data.title: Senator Seeks Explanation for SEC Director's Resignation Amid Crypto Cases
data.description: Senator Richard Blumenthal has requested SEC Chair Paul Atkins to clarify the circumstances surrounding the resignation of enforcement director Margaret "Meg" Ryan. According to NS3.AI, Blumenthal's letter, sent on Monday, seeks records related to SEC cryptocurrency cases and communications involving Ryan and senior agency officials. Ryan stepped down on March 16 after serving slightly more than six months in her position.

data.title: Ran Neuner Questions Bitcoin's Core Narrative Amid Market Uncertainty
data.description:
Ran Neuner, a prominent figure in the crypto industry, has raised questions about Bitcoin's fundamental narrative. According to Cointelegraph, Neuner expressed difficulty in articulating why individuals should invest in Bitcoin, highlighting a significant challenge in defining its identity. Initially promoted as peer-to-peer money and later as digital gold, Bitcoin's role has become increasingly ambiguous, particularly after its failure to align with traditional store-of-value assets like gold in recent cycles.

Neuner's uncertainty about Bitcoin's value proposition contributes to a broader discussion on the future direction of the crypto market as 2026 approaches. Rather than making bold predictions about Bitcoin's price, Neuner advises investors to avoid speculating on market trends. Instead, he emphasizes the importance of developing data-driven strategies and safeguarding against potential losses. This perspective shifts the focus from speculative forecasts to a more analytical approach to investment.

The conversation also touches on macroeconomic factors influencing market behavior, such as the Iran conflict, fluctuating oil prices, and inflation. Neuner suggests that capital flows, rather than media headlines, provide the most reliable insights in an increasingly complex information landscape. Additionally, he envisions a future where AI agents conduct transactions autonomously, potentially leading to a new digital economy powered by crypto infrastructure. This vision underscores the transformative potential of technology in reshaping economic interactions.

data.title: Bitcoin Accumulation Surges as Miners' Position Index Declines
data.description: Bitcoin accumulation addresses have significantly increased their holdings, adding approximately 67,000 BTC over the past week. According to NS3.AI, CryptoQuant data reveals that balances rose from 138,000 BTC on March 23 to around 205,000 BTC by March 30. Meanwhile, the 30-day Miners’ Position Index has dropped to -1.042, indicating a shift in miner behavior. Additionally, Binance's seven-day net taker flow turned negative, reaching $1.2 billion on Monday, suggesting changes in market dynamics.

data.title: White House Warns of Escalation Against Iran Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: The White House has issued a warning of potential escalation in attacks on Iran, targeting critical civilian energy infrastructure. Bloomberg posted on X that this development comes as the conflict enters its fifth week, causing significant disruptions in global markets. The situation remains tense with no immediate signs of resolution, impacting various sectors worldwide.

data.title: Bluesky's Attie App Faces Significant User Blocks Since Launch
data.description: Bluesky's AI-powered feed-building application, Attie, has been blocked 125,000 times since its public release on Saturday. According to NS3.AI, this figure places Attie just behind U.S. Vice President JD Vance in terms of the most-blocked accounts on the platform, surpassing both the White House and ICE.
